figures.caption: Figure 2. Visualization of LMFT on four videos (two left, two right). Each video has three rows: original frames, motion differences, and LMFT processed frames. LMFT selects action-relevant patches, dropping static or low-motion background patches for effective DA.
Table 1. VUDA results on Daily-DA. Colored rows show our results, and the results in other rows are taken from [16] and [22].
Table 2. VUDA results on UCF↔HMDBfull. Rows in colors are our experimental results, and others are from [16] and [22].
Table 4. Training efficiency of token reduction methods.
Table 5. Inference efficiency of token reduction methods.

Figure 1. Overview of LMFT. For both source and target videos, LMFT tokenizes frames into patch tokens, computes the L1 distance between consecutive temporal tokens, and discards those with differences below a learnable threshold τ , which primarily correspond to redundant and static background regions. The remaining motion-rich action-relevant tokens are then fed into the ViT-based DA framework.
